facts.claim: Hierarchical multi-tenant RBAC system: 3-level org hierarchy (Empresa > Estabelecimento > Setor) with downward permission cascade; user's effective permissions = union of group-assigned perms at requested level + inherited parent perms

Django-based RBAC with 2-stage permission resolution: (1) PermissionsChoices catalog → codename→bool payload (RULE-001), (2) scope-dispatch via TrilhasPermissaoMixin resolving empresa/estabelecimento/setor scopes with per-HTTP-method permission gating (RULE-009)

Frontend SSR guard (validateRoute) with critical auth defect: token variable initialized as {} (object) not '' (string), rendering token-presence check dead code; default call pattern validateRoute(ctx) with no permission argument bypasses authentication entirely on 19+ pages

Permission payload filtering: GrupoAcesso.to_representation suppresses usuarios array on list/destroy (performance); UsuarioSerializer.to_representation filters setores to current empresa (multi-tenant scoping, absent if no request context)

Multiple named permission predicates for different access patterns: super-admin (is_chatbot_superuser), partner-required (request.partner), owner-only (user.id == obj.owner), authenticated (non-anonymous), empresa read/write (identical implementation)
Post-login routing: 0 empresas → error + logout; 1 empresa → auto-select + redirect to /empresa/{id}; >1 empresa → company selector screen; refreshToken called only for 1+ empresa branches

load_bearing_rules.summary: Hierarchical permission cascade: setor inherits estabelecimento, which inherits empresa; effective perms = union of group-assigned + inherited
Scope-based RBAC dispatch with per-method permission gating; resolves empresa/estabelecimento/setor scope and validates permission membership
CRITICAL DEFECT: validateRoute SSR guard token var = {} (always truthy); default call pattern validateRoute(ctx) skips auth on 19+ pages
GrupoAcesso permission catalog: PermissionsChoices → codename→bool payload, defaulting all False, flipping to True only if assigned AND in catalog
User cargos lookup empresa-scoped: returns cargo values from GrupoAcesso records for user within empresa, null if no empresa in context
DISCREPANCY: LeituraEmpresa and ReadAndWriteEmpresa classes identical (both check user.is_anonymous); no write-specific gating
Post-login dispatch: 0 empresas → logout; 1 → auto-select; >1 → selector screen
Admin sidebar menu gating: can_manage_empresa → 4 items (empresa, estab, setores, leitos); can_manage_grupo_acesso → grupos; can_manage_usuario → profissionais
GrupoAcesso queryset scope resolution: setor > estabelecimento > empresa priority; soft-deleted excluded via objects_without_deleted
AMBIGUOUS: cargo enumeration mismatch — backend model (select_related, 1-62) vs frontend cargosObject map (display, 64-87)
DISCREPANCY: External SSO fallback with auto-provisioning; TODO comment 'Adicionar verificação se tem acesso ao projeto Trilhas' not implemented

systemic_issues.title: SSR Authentication Gate Defect (CRITICAL)
systemic_issues.description: Front-end SSR guard validateRoute() initializes token as {} (object literal, always truthy) instead of '' or null. Default call pattern validateRoute(ctx) (no permission argument) renders 19+ pages with NO effective server-side authentication check. Rendering depends on downstream client-side API calls for actual auth enforcement, out of FE partition scope.

systemic_issues.title: Permission Naming vs Implementation Mismatch
systemic_issues.description: Read vs read-write empresa permissions (LeituraEmpresa, ReadAndWriteEmpresa) defined as distinct classes but implemented identically—both check only is_anonymous, no write-specific gating. Access-level enumeration (read/read-write) appears dormant (RULE-047).

systemic_issues.title: Permission Reuse Without Clear Semantics
systemic_issues.description: Bed-management (leitos) page gated by can_manage_grupo_acesso (access-group permission) rather than bed-specific or can_manage_empresa. May be intentional coarse-grained admin permission or copy-paste artifact.

systemic_issues.title: UI-Only Restrictions Without Server-Side Enforcement
systemic_issues.description: Dashboard analítico and Inconsistências buttons shown only when empresa.tipo === 'automatica', but destination pages use default validateRoute(ctx) with no server-side tipo check. Restriction bypassable via direct URL navigation.

systemic_issues.title: Enumeration Divergence (Backend Model vs Frontend Copy)
systemic_issues.description: cargo enumeration (RULE-044): backend select_related list vs frontend cargosObject map. Role codes (RULE-045): backend model codes (proprietario/usuario/monitor) vs frontend type definitions diverge; recorded as AMBIGUOUS pending reconciliation.

systemic_issues.title: Validation Gaps in Serializers
systemic_issues.description: LoginSerializer has required-field validation rules declared but enforcement never triggered: core/api/v1/views/login.py lines 29-34 use request.data.get() directly, bypassing serializer validation entirely.

systemic_issues.title: Typo in Method-Exception Configuration
systemic_issues.description: exceto_metodo parameter misspells PATCH as PATH across multiple viewsets (UsuarioViewSet, GrupoAcessoViewSet, others), though actual method matching may be case-insensitive or corrected in framework layer.

systemic_issues.title: Scope Membership Pre-Validation Defect
systemic_issues.description: GrupoAcesso creation accepts request.data['usuario_ids'] with no pre-check that users belong to the target scope. Code contains *kwargs typo (latent defect).

core_terms.cargo: job/role title, assigned within a GrupoAcesso context
core_terms.grupo: access group, links users to permissions via Django Permission model
core_terms.estabelecimento: establishment/facility, middle level, belongs to empresa
core_terms.setor: sector/ward, lowest level, belongs to estabelecimento
core_terms.escopo: scope context for permission resolution (empresa, estabelecimento, setor)

open_questions: Reconciliation needed: cargo backend select list vs frontend cargosObject map (RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-044)
Clarification: intentional coarse-grained admin permission reuse (bed-management on can_manage_grupo_acesso) vs copy-paste (RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-021)
Root-cause: Why validateRoute token guard initialized as {} instead of empty string (RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-020)
Verification: Is exceto_metodo PATCH/PATH misspelling corrected in framework layer (RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-056)
Scope enforcement: Should GrupoAcesso user creation validate that users already belong to target scope (RULE-AUTH-USUARIOS-051)
notes: Non-clinical RBAC cluster (Django Permission-based) with 63 rules: 51 OK, 9 DISCREPANCY, 3 AMBIGUOUS. Two verdict categories: 61 NOT_APPLICABLE (non-clinical), 2 UNVERIFIABLE (proprietary internal rules). Highest-impact finding: SSR authentication gate defect in validateRoute enables 19+ pages to render without server-side auth check; real exposure depends on backend API enforcement. PT-BR vocabulary rich in organizational hierarchy and permission naming; all core terms preserved verbatim for v2 design.
